title: How Long And Dreary Is The Night.
content:
To a Gaelic air.


I.

        How long and dreary is the night
            When I am frae my dearie!
        I sleepless lie frae e'en to morn,
            Tho' I were ne'er sae weary.
        I sleepless lie frae e'en to morn,
            Tho' I were ne'er sae weary.

II.

        When I think on the happy days
            I spent wi' you, my dearie,
        And now what lands between us lie,
            How can I but be eerie!
        And now what lands between us lie,
            How can I be but eerie!

III.

        How slow ye move, ye heavy hours,
            As ye were wae and weary!
        It was na sae ye glinted by,
            When I was wi' my dearie.
        It was na sae ye glinted by,
            When I was wi' my dearie.
meaning:
"How Long And Dreary Is The Night." is a quintessential example of Robert Burns's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
